idea-plugin.name: GitHub Dark Pure Black
idea-plugin.description:
GitHub Dark — Pure Black

A no-compromise pure black (#000000) variant of GitHub Dark for IntelliJ Platform IDEs. Built to feel like a TUI — flat, quiet, zero chrome tint.

What's pure black

  • Editor pane, gutter, console, terminal
  • All tool windows (Project view, Terminal, Services, Build, Run, Debug, Commit)
  • Tab bar, main toolbar, status bar, popups, dialogs, menus
  • Text fields, combo boxes, dropdowns

Visual separation

Subtle #30363d 1px borders between panels so you can tell them apart without sacrificing the flat black look. Uses GitHub Primer border.default.

Bundled bonuses (opt-in)

  • Keymap — "Pure Black Mac" (Mac OS X with two tweaks: Cmd+Enter for Claude Code GUI chat newline, Alt+N for New Element menu)
  • Code style — "Pure Black Default" (language-standard indent: spaces everywhere, PEP 8 for Python, rustfmt for Rust, 2-space for HCL/Terraform/Protobuf/prototext)

First-run experience

On first install, a one-time notification offers to apply everything at once: the theme, editor color scheme, keymap, block cursor, indent guides off, intention bulb off. Click Apply all or Dismiss. Non-intrusive, never asks twice.

Parent

Extends IntelliJ's built-in Islands Dark. Fully compatible with the modern Islands UI.

Fonts

Bundled editor scheme references Fira Code. Install via brew install --cask font-fira-code on macOS. If not installed, IDEA falls back to another monospace.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.2.9

  • Warning and weak-warning ranges now show a dotted amber underline in the main editor, distinct from the error wave underline. Side error-stripe markers stay amber.

1.2.8

  • Warning and weak-warning ranges no longer draw yellow outlines in the main editor; amber error-stripe markers stay visible on the side.

1.2.7

  • Warning and weak-warning ranges no longer paint yellow blocks in the main editor; underline/effect and error-stripe markers stay amber.

1.2.6

  • Apply all now disables IntelliJ's File Colors master toggle — no more green tint on test folders, grey on non-project files, etc. Matches the pure-black aesthetic. Folder role markers (excluded, generated) are unaffected.

1.2.5

  • Fixed: Rust enum / type alias / macro-call / lifetime / trait-method tokens no longer inherit italic from the Rust plugin's Darcula overlay — explicit FONT_TYPE=0 on all org.rust.* overrides. Enum variants now render in constant blue, lifetimes in keyword coral.

1.2.4

  • De-branded for public release: keymap renamed "Pure Black Mac", code style renamed "Pure Black Default" (also reworked to language-standard defaults: spaces everywhere, PEP 8 Python, rustfmt Rust, 2-space HCL/Protobuf/prototext).
  • Editor color scheme Rust token overrides realigned with authentic GitHub Dark: constants + static use the constant blue (`#79c0ff`) instead of the type purple; italics removed from enum / type alias / macro.
  • First-run notification text rewritten to neutral wording.

1.2.3

  • Fixed: first-run Apply all actually switches keymap to "Vedhavyas Mac" (bundledKeymap expects bare filename, registers by filename not XML name attribute — renamed resource accordingly)

1.2.1

  • Fixed: first-run Apply all reliably switches editor scheme (direct getScheme(name) lookup + iterate-all fallback)

1.2.0

  • Added first-run notification that applies all defaults in one click: theme, editor scheme, keymap, block cursor on, indent guides off, intention bulb off
  • Persistent state — notification shows once, never again

1.1.0

  • Added bundled keymap "Vedhavyas Mac"
  • Added bundled code style "Vedhavyas Default"

1.0.0

  • Initial release — pure-black UI theme + editor color scheme + border accents
